title: Getting Data Out (Returns) 📤
description: Make your functions calculate answers and hand them back.

slides.title: Pushing Data Out 📤
slides.content:
# Return Types

Functions don't just take data IN. They can also push data OUT!

If you want a function to calculate an answer and hand it back to you, you must declare a **return type** using an arrow `->` right before the curly braces.

```swift
func getPi() -> Double {
    return 3.14159
}
```
Here, the function promises to return a `Double`.

quizData.question: What symbol is used to indicate that a function will push data out (a Return Type)?
quizData.explanation: Swift uses a single hyphen and a greater-than sign `->` to visually indicate 'Output' from a function.

slides.title: Capturing the Output 🪤

quizData.question: If a function is defined as `func isAdult(age: Int) -> Bool`, what MUST the function do?

options.text: It must use the 'return' keyword to spit out a true or false value.

quizData.explanation: Once you promise a return type with `->`, Swift explicitly forces you to `return` a value of that exact type, or your code won't compile.

slides.title: Early Exit 🏃‍♂️
